title: Scaffold Node/TypeScript project
description: package.json, tsconfig, build/test/lint tooling, dir layout. Root dependency for all code. Target stack per SPEC §3.2: Node/TS, Agent SDK, Slack Bolt, SQLite, stdio MCP.
close_reason: Scaffold complete: pnpm+Vitest+Biome, ESM, Node22, TS 5.9.3 strict/NodeNext, dir skeleton, smoke test green. Commits 612e715 (scaffold) + ef1c356 (pinning fixes). Review clean after fix loop.

title: Project Foundation & Runtime
description: Greenfield scaffold and the daemon runtime host. Owns: Node/TypeScript project setup (build/test/lint), Claude Agent SDK harness (stdio MCP), config system (pinned week-boundary TZ + trigger time, per-model rates, cook-night counts), secrets loading (1Password service account primary, chmod-600 env fallback), dev/prod profile switch (channel ID + separate SQLite path + force-regenerate + post/dry-run), and the launchd daemon plist (boot-launch + KeepAlive) plus the in-process Sunday scheduler. Root dependency for all other epics. Source: SPEC §3.2, §7, §9.1, §9.4.
close_reason: Project Foundation & Runtime complete: all 7 tasks done (scaffold, config, Agent SDK harness, dev/prod profile, secrets, daemon+scheduler, harness hardening). Reviewed via SDD; 79 tests green on build/phase-1-foundation.

title: Read NoteStore hashtags as authoritative recipe metadata (course/quality/season/effort/diet)
description: SPEC §5.2 designs planning around human-maintained Notes tags, but v1.0 never read them (LLM re-inferred season/effort/quality/veg and was blind to #side). Osascript can't expose hashtags; they live in NoteStore.sqlite. Implemented: a read-only NoteStore hashtag reader (notes-tags.ts) + pure tag->metadata mapping (tag-metadata.ts); merged at query time in search.ts (tags authoritative for course/quality/season/effort + veg positive-override; LLM still owns times/ingredients); structured-store stores tags independently (upsertTags, tags_json col + migration); sync refreshes tags every pass (cheap, no re-extraction); pools.ts excludes non-dinner courses (#side/#dessert/#breakfast/#appetizer). Verified live on the 50-recipe index: potato salad #side excluded from mains; plan rationales now cite real #N-stars quality.
acceptance_criteria: Sides never selected as standalone dinners; tags authoritative where present with LLM fallback; NoteStore read fails soft. DONE + live-verified.
close_reason: Implemented + live-verified: NoteStore hashtag reader (notes-tags.ts) + pure tag->metadata (tag-metadata.ts); tags merged at query time in search (authoritative for course/quality/season/effort, veg positive-override, LLM fallback); structured-store stores tags independently (upsertTags + tags_json migration); sync refreshes tags every pass (no re-extraction); pools exclude non-dinner courses. Verified: #side potato salad excluded from mains; rationales cite real #N-stars. 454 tests. Merged PR #9. Follow-ups: slim extraction prompt (drop tag-owned fields); side+main pairing.

title: Planner selection prompt omits the WeekPlan output shape (+ slot_type mismatch) -> plan generation fails
description: Discovered 2026-07-16 running the real planner against a 50-recipe index (post q95.9/q95.10). buildSelectionPrompt (src/planner/input.ts) OUTPUT section only says 'Emit a single WeekPlan JSON object and nothing else' — it never specifies the actual shape, so the model invents one: it returned {"week_plan": {...}} (extra envelope) with guessed field names, failing WeekPlanSchema (.strict) validation: 'week_key expected string received undefined; meals expected array received undefined; Unrecognized key: week_plan'. The one bounded repair also failed. Two more concrete mismatches: (a) SLOTS/prompt tells the model to tag slot_type 'weeknight'/'weekend', but SelectedMealSchema.slot_type enum is 'constrained'/'relaxed'; (b) the week_key value is never rendered into the prompt, so the model cannot echo it. Unit tests never caught this because select/validate tests mock llm.runQuery with already-correct JSON. Impact: plan generation fails on the real path — blocks producing any weekly plan (go-live).
acceptance_criteria: buildSelectionPrompt states the EXACT WeekPlan JSON shape (root week_key + meals[] + optional summary; per-meal slot_type constrained|relaxed, recipe_id, title, day:null, veg discriminated union {inherent|separable+note|second_dish+recipe_id+title}, flags[], rationale) and includes the actual week_key value; slot_type wording matches the schema enum. A live selection call against the real index returns a schema-valid WeekPlan that renders a plan.
notes: Fix is in the prompt (input.ts), not the schema. Verify live by generating a plan against the local index. Consider adding a compact shape/example block mirroring buildExtractionPrompt's explicit-shape style.
close_reason: Fixed: buildSelectionPrompt now specifies the exact WeekPlan output shape (root week_key with real value + meals[] + summary?; per-meal slot_type constrained|relaxed, recipe_id, title, day:null, veg union, flags[], rationale) and forbids the week_plan envelope; SLOTS wording uses the real enum. Root cause was an unspecified output shape + slot_type weeknight/weekend vs schema constrained/relaxed + missing week_key. Verified live against the 50-recipe index: selection returns a schema-valid WeekPlan that renders a full 6-meal veg-covered plan. Merged PR #8.

title: Extraction returns empty fields at ~20k input tokens/note on real corpus (Agent SDK overhead?)
description:
Discovered 2026-07-15 via the first real end-to-end 'pnpm sync' (after the q95.9 reader fix), on 20 real recipe notes ('Untagged Recipes'). extractRecipeFields 'succeeded' for all 20 (extractionFailures=0, needs_review=0) but stored DEGENERATE fields: time {active:null,total:null,prep:null,confidence:0}, ingredients:[], effort_tags:[], season_tags:[], veg_status:'contains_meat', quality:'untested'. i.e. the model returned a valid-SHAPED but empty extraction.

Cost/usage were anomalous: [sync] spend tokens=411214 (in=411090 out=124) $0.8234 for 20 notes => ~20,554 INPUT tokens/note and only ~6 OUTPUT tokens/note. The extraction prompt (buildExtractionPrompt) is ~1k tokens incl. the note body (~450 tokens avg), so ~20k input is ~20x expected. Hypothesis: llm.runQuery goes through the Claude Agent SDK (src/llm/agent-sdk-client.ts), which likely injects a large default system prompt / tool context per call, inflating input and steering the model toward an empty/no-op response. The tiny (6 tok) output also suggests the response isn't the full JSON we think, or usage accounting via meteredLlmClient/the SDK is off.

Impact (BLOCKER): empty structured fields mean the planner's hard filters can't work — no active-time (<60) filter, no veg_status => the vegetarian-daughter HARD constraint can't be satisfied, no ingredients. Plans built on this are useless. Also cost: ~$0.04/note x 798 ~= $33 for a full sync, which would blow the $2 per-run cap (MP_GENERATION_DOLLAR_CAP) after ~48 notes.
acceptance_criteria: Extraction on real notes returns populated fields (real times, ingredients, correct veg_status) verified against a sample of the corpus; input tokens/note are in the low thousands (no giant hidden system prompt); a full 798-note sync is affordable and completes within a sane cap. Investigate whether the Agent SDK is the right transport for a single-shot extraction call vs. a leaner Messages-API path, or whether the SDK needs configuring (system prompt, no tools) for this use.
notes: Reproduce: MP_RECIPES_FOLDER='Untagged Recipes' pnpm sync (costs ~$0.8). Inspect structured_fields.fields_json in data/recipe-index.sqlite. Related: q95.6 (extraction-quality validation on real corpus). Start by logging the actual raw LLM response text + the exact request the Agent SDK sends (system prompt, tools) for one note.
close_reason: Root cause: agent-sdk-client left settingSources unset, so the SDK loaded the dev's CLAUDE.md + .claude settings + default tools into every call (~22k input tokens, 13x blowup) and framed the model as a coding agent => empty/degenerate extraction. Fixed: settingSources:[] always + tools:[] for plain completions. Also fixed usage accounting (was summing under-reporting assistant turns => ~6 output tokens/note; now reads authoritative cache-inclusive usage from the result message). Verified live: input ~1.6k, extraction returns correct populated fields (veg_status/ingredients/times). Merged PR #7 (dea2845). Follow-ups: q95.11 (cache-aware cost rates); initial full sync ~$21 for 798 notes needs a raised MP_GENERATION_DOLLAR_CAP.

title: notes-reader too slow/unreliable on real corpus (798-note Recipes folder) — blocks sync
description:
Discovered 2026-07-15 while verifying go-live (bj1.1) against the real Backhaushold Notes. The 'Recipes' folder exists and is correctly named (798 notes; MP_RECIPES_FOLDER default is right), but readNotes cannot practically read it:

1. Per-note property access: notes-reader's JXA does notes.map(n => ({id:n.id(), title:n.name(), body:n.body(), modifiedAt:n.modificationDate()})) = 4 Apple Events x N notes. A read-only readNotes('Recipes') did NOT complete in >3 min (effectively hangs). The first 'pnpm sync' returned total=0 in ~7 min with $0 spend — most likely the macOS TCC automation prompt (first node->osascript access) was pending, so osascript returned empty; the reader silently yields [] on a non-match.
2. Bulk property access (f.notes.body() across all notes) throws AppleEvent -1741 (~5s), so the naive one-shot batch fix needs type-coercion / paging care.
3. A JSON.parse failure was hit reading the 34-note 'Drinks' folder via readNotes (osascript output failed the reader's parse) — suggests some note content or the arg-passing path breaks JSON emission.
4. Folder matching is whitespace-sensitive: 'Untagged Recipes ' (trailing space) silently returns 0 notes in 85ms.

Impact: recipe sync cannot populate the index from the real corpus -> no real plan can be generated -> BLOCKS go-live end-to-end verification (bj1.1/first prod run). Note that readNotes runs on EVERY sync (before the hash gate), so this cost is paid weekly, not just once.
acceptance_criteria: readNotes('Recipes') reliably returns all 798 notes in a bounded, reasonable time (e.g. paged bulk property reads, correct type coercion to avoid -1741); robust JSON emission for arbitrary note bodies; folder-name matching trims/normalizes whitespace; a read timeout + progress so a hang is surfaced not silent. 'pnpm sync' then populates the index end-to-end.
notes: Evidence gathered via osascript probes: app-level whose({name:'Recipes'}) matches 1 folder / 798 notes instantly (counts only), but iterating bodies is the bottleneck. Consider paging notes in chunks and reading id/name/body/modificationDate as bulk arrays per chunk.
close_reason: Fixed + verified live: notes-reader bulk-reads id/name/modificationDate/plaintext (was per-property-per-note => ~4min hang returning 0), uses plaintext() not image-laden body() HTML, trims folder-name matching, adds 180s timeout + 64MB maxBuffer. readNotes('Recipes') -> 798 notes in 0.8s; end-to-end pnpm sync on 'Untagged Recipes' stored all 20. Merged PR #6 (eea6237). NOTE: live sync surfaced a separate extraction problem (empty fields, high token cost) — see q95.6.

title: Wire recipe sync (syncNotes) into daemon boot/trigger path
description: syncNotes (src/recipe-mcp/sync.ts) has no non-test caller. The daemon's trigger path (composeDaemon -> buildPlanFor -> composePools/search) queries the vector store directly and never syncs from Apple Notes first, so the weekly plan is built against whatever is already in ./data/*.sqlite. SPEC weekly flow requires 'sync recipes via Recipe MCP -> generate plan'. Need: build SyncDeps (readNotes via notes-reader, embedder, VectorStore as SyncStore, StructuredStore, metered llm) in main(), and call syncNotes at the start of onTrigger (before buildPlan) and/or provide a standalone 'pnpm sync' entrypoint for the initial corpus load. Until this lands, going live requires a manual one-off sync to populate the index.
acceptance_criteria: onTrigger (or an explicit pre-trigger step) runs syncNotes before buildPlan against the real Apple Notes source; initial corpus can be populated on a fresh machine; sync failures are isolated per ADR 0001 and don't abort the daemon.
notes: Discovered 2026-07-13 while writing the go-live runbook. Blocks a *useful* prod run (daemon boots + posts fine, but against an empty/stale recipe index on a fresh machine).
close_reason: Wired syncNotes into the daemon (makeBuildPlanWithSync runs sync before buildPlan, once per real generation, proceed+alert on failure) + added standalone `pnpm sync` (sync-cli.ts). Shared runSync factory. MP_RECIPES_FOLDER env var. 430 tests, CI green, merged to main via PR #3.

title: Daemon-boot integration: wire generateForWeek + onStartup into the daemon
description: Composition root (src/index.ts) that constructs the REAL deps and wires them into the E1 daemon: load config → resolve profile → load secrets (applySecretsToEnv) → open recipe stores (VectorStore/StructuredStore) + embedder + LlmClient → bind search_recipes + get_recipe → open SessionStore(profile.sqlitePath) → build a dry-run/profile-aware post (renders WeekPlan via the E5 render, respects postMode, returns a synthetic ts in dry-run) + a console alert placeholder (E6 later) → assemble generateForWeek + onStartup deps → runDaemon({onStartup, onTrigger: fire generateForWeek(currentPlanWeek), fireOnStart}). Real Slack post (E5 bj1.3) and #agent-alerts (E6) swap in later. Makes the tested units into a bootable daemon.
close_reason: Daemon-boot integration complete: pure composeDaemon factory (builds real generateForWeek/onStartup, force from profile, currentPlanWeek trigger) + main() bootstrap (real stores/embedder/LlmClient/bound search+getRecipe/buildPlan/SessionStore, dry-run post via renderPlan + synthetic ts, console alert). Placeholders removed. Commit 816626e. Review Approved (wiring field-accurate, gates re-run; household nit Low/non-blocking).

title: DECISION: 1Password service-account availability for boot secrets
description: SPEC §9.1 / §10.3 open item. Confirm the op CLI / 1Password supports service accounts readable at daemon boot (no interactive unlock), else fold setup into the build or use the env-file fallback. EXTERNAL LEAD TIME — start early. Gates secrets loading (T1 secrets). Ratify: bd close --reason.
design:
RECOMMENDATION: 1Password service account as primary boot-secret source; chmod-600 env-file fallback sanctioned. VERIFIED against official docs (developer.1password.com → 1password.dev, fetched 2026-07-11):

- Fully non-interactive: setting OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN authenticates the `op` CLI with NO interactive unlock — exactly the launchd-at-boot path this needs. `op read op://vault/item/field` works with the token alone. CONFIRMS the SPEC §9.1 assumption.
- Rate limits: service accounts have hourly/daily request caps. One `op read` = 3 requests by default; pass explicit vault + item IDs to cut it to 1. v1.0 reads 2 secrets at startup → trivial (≤6 req/boot, or 2 with IDs). Non-issue.
- Caveat: OP_CONNECT_HOST / OP_CONNECT_TOKEN take precedence over OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN — ensure they are unset in the daemon env.
- Residual boot caveat (already noted in SPEC §9.1): the SA token itself must be readable at boot. This does not eliminate the root-secret problem, it narrows it to one rotatable/auditable token — provide it via the launchd plist EnvironmentVariables or a chmod-600 file the daemon reads at startup.

NET: no blocker to making 1Password SA primary. Implementation notes for T1 secrets: use vault+item IDs in `op read` calls; clear OP_CONNECT_* in the daemon env; provision OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN outside any repo. Env-file fallback remains available (v1.0 needs only Slack bot token + Anthropic key).

To ratify: bd close meal-planner-njt --reason "..."  (auto-unblocks T1 secrets once scaffold is also done).
close_reason: Ratified (fact verified against official 1Password docs 2026-07-11): 1Password service account is the primary boot-secret source — OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN gives fully non-interactive op-CLI auth suitable for a launchd daemon at boot; rate limits are a non-issue for 2 startup secrets. chmod-600 env-file fallback remains sanctioned. Implementation notes captured in the decision design (use vault+item IDs; clear OP_CONNECT_*; provision token outside any repo).

title: DECISION: week_key format (Sunday-date vs ISO year-week)
description: ADR 0002 D1 open item. week_key must be pinned as either the anchor Sunday's date or ISO year-week. Gates the week_key computation (T3 week_key) and the session PRIMARY KEY. Ratify: bd close --reason.
design: RECOMMENDATION: anchor Sunday's date (e.g. '2026-07-12') in the pinned TZ. Rationale (ADR 0002 D1): dodges ISO's Monday/Sunday boundary confusion given a Sunday trigger; human-readable; sorts chronologically. Depends on the pinned TZ + trigger time config (T1 config). Ratify by closing.
close_reason: Ratified: week_key = anchor Sunday's date in the pinned TZ (e.g. '2026-07-12'), per ADR 0002 D1 — dodges ISO Monday/Sunday boundary confusion given the Sunday trigger, human-readable, sorts chronologically. Used as the session PRIMARY KEY. Depends on pinned TZ + trigger time (T1 config).

title: Per-run generation hard cap (~$2)
description: In-code hard cap ~$2 (~500K tokens) on a generation run — Anthropic offers alerts, not enforced cutoffs, so the orchestrator enforces its own. On cap hit: stop, alert #agent-alerts, never die silently. SPEC §9.3.
close_reason: Per-run $ cap complete: CostCapExceededError + enforcement in meteredLlmClient (after-record, strict >, capUsd=config.generationDollarCap), reuses existing generateForWeek failure/alert path (generate.ts 0 diff lines — verified), CostMeter now names model in error. Commit 2517446. Review Approved (minor: boundary test + index smoke gap, tracked).

title: Token/$ tracking across a run
description: Accumulate tokens (returned per response) across all Agent SDK loop calls within one run; convert to $ via per-model rates in config; persist to session counters (token_spend/cost_usd). A run aggregates across all calls, not per-call. SPEC §9.3.
close_reason: Token/$ tracking complete: CostMeter + costUsd + meteredLlmClient decorator; generateForWeek persists per-run token_spend/cost_usd (reset-per-run, incl. failure path) via additive spendPatch; one shared meter threaded through composeDaemon. Commit 3fb7478. Review Approved (generate.ts verified byte-additive; crash-safety intact). Minor→fkg.2.

title: Post draft + capture thread ts
description: chat.postMessage to the profile's channel ID as a new top-level message (thread-per-week); capture parent thread ts (load-bearing). Called by generateForWeek between the 'generating' insert and the 'suggested' update. SPEC §7.
notes: bj1.1 (create Slack app) is a RUNTIME prerequisite — provides the bot token (→ secrets) + channel IDs (→ profile). The posting CODE is built here with @slack/web-api mocked; it actually posts once the user creates the app and supplies the token. Not a build-time blocker.
close_reason: Slack poster complete: SlackPoster (Web API chat.postMessage, injectable client, renderPlan text, returns ts) + main() post-mode wiring (post→real, dry-run→placeholder). Commits 7c11583 + a1d1663 (surface safe err.data.error code, no token leak — verified). Review Approved after fix. bj1.1 app-creation is user's runtime step.

title: Create Slack app + channels (bot token, chat:write)
description: New Slack app in Backhaushold workspace; bot token (xoxb) with chat:write ONLY — no Socket Mode / app-level token in v1.0. Channels: #meal-plan, #dev-meal-plan, #agent-alerts (#grocery-list is v4.0). SPEC §7, §9.2.
close_reason: Slack app created in Backhaushold (bot token, chat:write only — no Socket Mode); channels #meal-plan/#dev-meal-plan/#agent-alerts created. Verified 2026-07-15: chat.postMessage to #dev-meal-plan returned ok:true (op:// refs resolve, token+scope+membership work). Caveat: only #dev-meal-plan posting was exercised; confirm the bot is also invited to #meal-plan and #agent-alerts before the first prod run.

title: WeekPlan → Slack markdown render
description: Deterministic render of WeekPlan to Slack markdown (selection≠presentation, ADR 0003 D3): surface per-meal veg path, do-ahead/untested flags, rationale, summary. LLM never writes the post. ADR 0003 D3.
close_reason: WeekPlan→markdown render complete: pure deterministic renderPlan(EnrichedWeekPlan)→mrkdwn, slot-grouped, all VegPath kinds, flags/rationale/summary, newline-safe. Commit 984806c. Review Approved (minor: source-hint untested, no functional risk).

title: Post-validation + one bounded repair retry
description: validate(): counts match slots, every recipe_id (incl second_dish) a real pool member, veg path present+consistent (inherent/second_dish checked vs veg_status; separable accepted on model word v1.0), no duplicates, flag sanity. On fail: ONE repair re-prompt with violations, else throw → alert+fail run. ADR 0003 D5.
notes: From select review (8zs.3): WeekPlan schemas are not .strict() (unlike ExtractedFieldsSchema). Consider strict parsing here to reject hallucinated LLM keys, consistent with the codebase's LLM-output-validation rationale. Also: summarizeZodError is now duplicated in extraction.ts + select.ts — candidate to consolidate into src/lib/.
close_reason: Post-validation + bounded repair complete: validateWeekPlan (counts/membership-by-slot/veg-consistency/no-dupes-incl-second_dish/flag-sanity) + selectValidatedPlan (<=2 LLM calls, PlanValidationError secret-safe). .strict() WeekPlan; summarizeZodError consolidated to src/lib/zod-errors.ts. Commit 494e619. Review Approved (adversarially verified; test-coverage gaps only).

title: WeekPlan schema + single LLM selection call
description: WeekPlan structured output (meals[]: slot_type, recipe_id, title, day=null v1.0, veg VegPath, flags, rationale) == working_plan. Single llmSelect over cheap-tier candidates; LLM decides, no agentic loop. ADR 0003 D1/D3/D4.
close_reason: WeekPlan schema + single LLM selection complete: zod WeekPlan/SelectedMeal/VegPath (discriminated union, day:null) + llmSelect (one runQuery, JSON extract, shape-validate, PlanSelectionError no-leak). Shared json-extraction.ts refactor (byte-identical, no regression). Commit 44192a1. Review Approved (nits only).

title: PlannerInput assembly + prompt builder
description: Assemble PlannerInput (slots, pools, household prose incl. veg daughter HARD + picky youngest, current_season, untested_present) and build the selection prompt (TASK/HOUSEHOLD/SLOTS/CANDIDATES/RULES/OUTPUT). Signals by mechanism (D2). ADR 0003.
close_reason: PlannerInput + selection prompt complete: typed input (slots/pools/household/season/untested_present) + buildSelectionPrompt (TASK/HOUSEHOLD/SLOTS/CANDIDATES/RULES(HARD/SOFT/POOL)/OUTPUT), conditional untested+season clauses, candidates render soft-signals, household caller-supplied. Commit 1b9b265. Review Approved (Minor coverage nits only).

title: Deterministic pool composition
description: buildPlan step 1: weeknight pool (active_max:60, limit=constrained*4) + weekend pool (relaxed, limit=relaxed*4); ensureVegFloor top-up via veg_status:vegetarian; maybeInjectUntested at configured rate. All code-side policy, no LLM. ADR 0003 D1, §6.4.
close_reason: Deterministic pool composition complete: weeknight (active_max) + weekend pools, veg-floor top-up capped to deficit, best-effort untested injection, dedupe by id, no LLM. Commits a008ded + eddbc8b (veg-floor cap fix). Review Approved after fix loop.

title: Startup catch-up (three cases)
description: onStartup: no row past trigger → generate; live row (suggested/under_revision/committed) → resume; stale 'generating' → alert ONCE to #agent-alerts, set failed, never auto-repost/auto-generate. ADR 0002 D4.
close_reason: Startup catch-up (onStartup) complete: three ADR 0002 D4 cases — no-row-past-trigger→generate, no-row-before-trigger→noop, live→resumeQuietly, stale generating→alert-once+failed (no auto-repost, proven), terminal→noop. Single now(). Commit d34416f. Review Approved (unreachable-branch claim proven correct; nits only).

title: week_key computation (clock-derived)
description: currentPlanWeek(now,cfg): most-recent-Sunday-on-or-before in pinned TZ, gated on trigger time; previousPlanWeek; triggerMoment. 'Active week' is computed, never stored (ADR 0002 D3). Depends on config TZ/trigger.
close_reason: week_key computation complete: currentPlanWeek/previousPlanWeek/triggerMoment/isActiveWeek (luxon, DST-correct, injected clock). tz/DST independently verified by review. Commits a4cd9b9 + 863db25 (pinned trigger-boundary + UTC/local + Monday edge tests). Review Approved.

title: State machine + generateForWeek (idempotency + write-before-post)
description: State machine generating→suggested→under_revision→committed +failed +expired. generateForWeek: idempotency gate (skip if row exists unless force), expirePriorIfUncommitted piggyback, insert 'generating' BEFORE post, update 'suggested'+thread_ts+working_plan AFTER ts. ADR 0002 D1/D2.
close_reason: State machine + generateForWeek complete: ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS + guarded transition(), idempotency gate, write-before-post ordering (tested via post-time row assertion), failed/suggested paths, expiry piggyback. Commits 09184a3 + 93c6438 (fix: return 'generated' on recovered write not rethrow; guard cosmetic expiry; retry breadcrumb). Review Approved after fix. Low follow-ups → bd6.7.

title: SQLite session schema + migrations
description: session table keyed by week_key (status, thread_ts nullable, working_plan JSON with nullable per-meal day, turn_count, token_spend, cost_usd, timestamps) + idx_session_thread_ts. Cost counters colocated so §9.3 caps update transactionally. ADR 0002.
close_reason: SQLite session store complete: ADR 0002 schema + typed SessionStore (insert/get/update-partial/getByThreadTs/close), caller-supplied clock, JSON working_plan, parameterized SQL. Commits b49022a + be69552 (test strengthening: same-file reopen idempotency + falsifiable no-clobber partial update). Review Approved.

title: search_recipes tool (cheap fan-out)
description: Vector search over query + deterministic filter predicates: active_max (HARD; null/low-confidence time FAILS → fail-closed), veg_status, season, effort, exclude_ids (defined now, used v2.0), limit. Returns lightweight RecipeCandidate (NO ingredient block). ADR 0001 D2/D3.
close_reason: search_recipes complete: vector search + deterministic post-filters (fail-closed active_max w/ confidence threshold, veg_status/season/effort), lightweight RecipeCandidate (no ingredients), exclude_ids passthrough, over-fetch to refill limit. Commit 755d081. Review Approved (minor untested branch combos only).

title: Ingest-time extraction pass (hash + version gated)
description: Single LLM pass per note → {time{active,total,prep,confidence}, ingredients[], veg_status, effort_tags, season_tags, quality}. Cache keyed by note.id, gated on contentHash AND extractor_version (re-tuning forces clean re-extract). Runs at sync, not per plan-run. ADR 0001 D1.
notes: From schema review (q95.1): schema.ts zod objects are NOT .strict() (unlike config.ts), so unrecognized keys are silently stripped. When validating LLM extraction output here, consider .strict() (or an explicit allowlist) to catch hallucinated/drifted fields early rather than dropping them silently.
close_reason: Ingest-time extraction pass complete: extraction.ts (LLM→ExtractedFields, strict validation, 1 bounded repair retry) + structured-store.ts (note_id cache, hash+extractor_version gated) + sync wiring with per-note error isolation. Commit 9e1767b. Review Approved (Moderate/Minor findings → follow-up filed).

title: Apple Notes MCP source + vector index
description: Apple Notes MCP + harness; sync notes and build/refresh the local vector index. Owns index freshness. ADR 0001 D1.
close_reason: Implemented notes-reader (osascript/JXA), transformers.js embedder, sqlite-vec vector store, and hash-gated sync. 147 tests pass; typecheck/lint/build clean.

title: launchd daemon + in-process Sunday scheduler
description: launchd plist = boot-launch + KeepAlive only (no calendar job). In-process scheduler fires generation at the Sunday slot. Confirm system sleep disabled (pmset -g → sleep 0); do one real test-fire of the scheduler path. SPEC §3.2, §9.4.
notes: From secrets review (7js.3): the op-CLI execFile calls have no timeout — a hung op at boot would hang the daemon indefinitely. When wiring boot-time secret loading in this task, add a timeout (execFile {timeout} or Promise race) with a clear failure/alert path.
close_reason: Daemon + in-process scheduler complete: DST-correct croner scheduler with error containment, injected onTrigger/onStartup seams for E3, boot secret-load timeout, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY wiring, pmset sleep-check, launchd plist. Commits d8d1dbe + 160286c. Review Approved after fix loop (Important+Medium fixed).

title: Freeze ingredient + shared value-type schema
description: Frozen capture schema (ADR 0001 D4): Ingredient{raw always, name, prep separate, quantity(scalar|range|none), unit nullable, optional, alternatives, group, confidence, needs_review} + shared types Quantity/VegStatus/Quality/TimeFields. Capture≠aggregation; raw always kept.
close_reason: Frozen recipe schema complete: shared value types + Ingredient + two-tier RecipeCandidate/Recipe as zod schemas with z.infer TS types (single source of truth). Exact ADR 0001 fidelity, no package_size (ratified→raw). Commit 809d622. Review Approved (Minor nits only).

title: Claude Agent SDK harness
description: Wire the Agent SDK to drive Claude over local MCP (stdio). Model = claude-sonnet-5 medium effort, as per-context config (not hardcoded). Gotchas: no manual budget_tokens (use effort), no non-default temperature/top_p/top_k, tokenizer ~1.0-1.35x. SPEC §9.3, §3.2.
close_reason: Agent SDK harness complete: LlmClient seam (only src/llm imports the SDK), Sonnet-5 gotchas enforced via real assertions, usage aggregation across turns, SDK shape verified vs installed .d.ts. Commit 42f09c9. Review Approved.

title: dev/prod profile switch
description: Single --profile dev|prod bundles: target channel ID (explicit, never name lookup), separate SQLite path per profile, force-regenerate default (on in dev), post-vs-dry-run. SPEC §7.
close_reason: dev/prod profile switch complete: resolveProfile bundles channelId/sqlitePath/forceRegenerate/postMode with SPEC §7 isolation enforced (no cross-profile fallback, #name rejection, distinct sqlite paths, collision throws). Matches config.ts validation style. Commit fa631a7. Review Approved (Minor nits only).

title: Config system + boot validation
description: Load and validate config: pinned week-boundary TZ + trigger time (load-bearing for week_key — ADR 0002), per-model $ rates, cook-night counts (constrained/relaxed). Validate at boot; a wrong/unset TZ silently mis-keys weeks. SPEC §9.4, ADR 0002.
close_reason: Config system + boot validation complete: typed zod schema, strict IANA tz validation (Intl.DateTimeFormat), HH:MM trigger, exact SPEC §9.3 defaults, error aggregation. Commit 7e10ea3. Review Approved (Minor nits only, tracked for final review).

title: Secrets loading (1Password SA / env fallback)
description: 1Password service account primary (fetch at startup; daemon can't do interactive unlock at boot), chmod-600 env-file fallback. v1.0 needs only Slack bot token (xoxb) + Anthropic API key. SPEC §9.1.
close_reason: Secrets loading complete: 1Password SA (op read, OP_CONNECT_* cleared unconditionally) + env fallback, only the 2 v1.0 secrets, no-leak enforced (never reads rejection reason; adversarial test) + execFile args array. Commit b71640e. Review Approved (no Critical/High).

title: Planner
description: Hybrid selection (ADR 0003): the code guarantees, the LLM decides. Owns: deterministic pool composition (weeknight active_max pool + weekend pool, veg-floor top-up, untested injection — all code-side policy), a single LLM selection call over cheap-tier candidates with signals-by-mechanism (structured fields as data, household context + kid-friendliness as prose), the WeekPlan structured output (== working_plan; per-meal veg path inherent/separable/second_dish; day always null in v1.0), deterministic post-validation (counts, pool membership, veg consistency, no duplicates, flag sanity) with ONE bounded repair retry, and get_recipe enrich of the chosen. Depends on E2. Source: ADR 0003, SPEC §6.

title: Slack Posting & Draft Rendering
description: Outbound-only Slack for v1.0 (SPEC §7, §9.2). Owns: the new Slack app in the Backhaushold workspace (bot token, chat:write scope only — NO Socket Mode / app-level token in v1.0), posting the weekly draft via chat.postMessage as a new top-level message (thread-per-week), a deterministic WeekPlan→Slack-markdown render step (selection ≠ presentation, ADR 0003 D3), and capturing/persisting the parent thread ts (load-bearing for v3.0). Target channel resolved by explicit ID from the dev/prod profile (never a name lookup). Called by E3's generateForWeek. Source: SPEC §7, §9.2, ADR 0003 D3.

title: Orchestrator: State, Idempotency & Recovery
description: The daemon's core loop and durable state (ADR 0002). Owns: SQLite session schema (keyed by week_key), the state machine (generating→suggested→under_revision→committed, +failed +expired), week_key computation (currentPlanWeek: clock-derived anchor Sunday in pinned TZ), the generation idempotency gate, write-before-post ordering (insert 'generating' BEFORE Slack post; update to 'suggested'+thread_ts+working_plan AFTER ts returns), startup catch-up (three cases; stale 'generating' → alert-once-then-failed, never auto-repost), expired piggybacked on next generation, and the cost-counter columns (turn_count/token_spend/cost_usd). Calls E4 (buildPlan) then E5 (post). Source: ADR 0002, SPEC §8.

title: Recipe MCP Server
description: Custom Apple Notes MCP server + harness that we own (ADR 0001). Owns: local vector index, an ingest-time LLM extraction pass gated on content-hash + extractor_version, a cached structured-field store, the frozen ingredient capture schema (raw always kept; name/prep/quantity(range|none)/unit/optional/alternatives/group/confidence), and the two-tier tool interface — search_recipes (cheap fan-out; hard predicates active_max + veg_status, fail-closed on null/low-confidence time) and get_recipe (full incl. ingredient block, chosen only). Consumed by the Planner (E4). Ingredient block built in v1.0, fully consumed in v4.0. Source: ADR 0001, SPEC §5.

title: Scope recipe sync to the food-only Notes folder + purge already-indexed drinks
description: The Apple Notes 'Recipes' folder is a SMART folder aggregating 'Food' (764) and 'Drinks' (34); syncing it pulled 34 drinks (e.g. 'Christmas Mimosas!') into the recipe index, where they pass main_dinner_only (untagged notes are main_dinner_eligible=true) and get planned as dinners. Fix = target the food-only real folder instead. JXA flattens the folder tree, so 'Food' is directly addressable by bare name -> config-only, no reader code change. Supersedes the course-allow-list idea (requiring #dinner) since scoping the source folder is cleaner and cheaper.
acceptance_criteria: MP_RECIPES_FOLDER points at the food-only folder ('Food'); the 34 drink rows are removed from recipe-index.sqlite (count 798 -> 764); a this-week dry-run selects no beverages; .env.example + docs/RUNBOOK.md document that the folder must be the food-only source, not the aggregating smart folder.
notes: Real tree: Shared/Recipes 1/{Food:764, Drinks:34}; 'Recipes'(798) is the smart folder. Purge mechanism: stores have NO delete method (sync only upserts) -> one-off transactional SQL delete across notes/vec_notes/structured_fields, backed up first. FOLLOW-UP (separate issue): sync should prune notes no longer in the target folder / deleted from Notes, so stale rows don't linger.

title: Clean up 1Password secrets config: dedicated Meal-Planner vault + kebab-case naming + fresh OPSA
description:
Reorganize the meal-planner secrets in 1Password onto a clean, self-contained convention. Currently the items live in a shared vault with a 'Meal Planner' name prefix and non-kebab field names, and the service-account token was exposed in a terminal session (see notes), so it must be rotated. Rolling the rotation into a proper vault/naming cleanup avoids touching secrets twice.

Scope:
1. Create a DEDICATED '\''Meal-Planner'\'' 1Password vault (isolates these secrets; the vault name provides the namespace).
2. Rename items to kebab-case WITHOUT the '\''Meal Planner'\'' prefix (redundant now that they live in a dedicated vault) — e.g. '\''anthropic-api-key'\'', '\''slack-bot-token'\''.
3. Rename fields to kebab-case.
4. Create a NEW OPSA (1Password Service Account) scoped to ONLY the '\''Meal-Planner'\'' vault (least-privilege), replacing the exposed token. Revoke the old OPSA.
5. Update the op:// references in the .env (MP_OP_ANTHROPIC_KEY_REF, MP_OP_SLACK_TOKEN_REF) and OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN to match the new vault/item/field paths, and update docs/RUNBOOK.md's secrets section accordingly.
acceptance_criteria: '\''Meal-Planner'\'' vault exists holding kebab-case items (no name prefix) with kebab-case fields; a new least-privilege OPSA scoped to that vault is issued and the old (exposed) token revoked; .env op:// refs + OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN updated and a boot (pnpm sync or daemon) successfully loads secrets via the new refs; docs/RUNBOOK.md secrets section reflects the new naming.
notes: Context: the OLD OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN was accidentally printed to a terminal session on 2026-07-16 (shell-quoting mistake during a status check), so rotation is required regardless of the naming cleanup — this chore bundles the two so secrets are touched once. loadSecrets() (src/secrets/secrets.ts) reads MP_OP_*_REF via 'op read' on the 1Password path (auto-selected when OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN is set); only the op:// ref STRINGS change, no code change expected. Verify the new OPSA has read access to the new vault before revoking the old one.

title: Wire v1.0 tag-based seasonality: derive current season from the clock and feed the planner
description: v1.0 tag-based seasonality is specified (SPEC §116/§151/§204, ADR-0003 §60/§104/§116-118) but only HALF-BUILT and untracked: the consumption side exists (search_recipes 'season' hard filter; the selection prompt's 'respect current season' soft bias; pools.ts threads cfg.season/current_season), but nothing ever SETS it. There is no 'season' field on Config and no derivation of the current season from the date/timezone, so cfg.season/current_season are always undefined and seasonality is dormant. Observed live 2026-07-16: 'Chicken Khao Soi' (tagged winter/fall/spring, NOT summer) was selected for a July week because season played no role — it survived on quality(5)+veg+variety alone. pools.ts even carries a comment: 'season isn't yet a field on Config (no current-season signal is wired up).' Scope: derive one of {spring,summer,fall,winter} from the current date in the configured MP_TIMEZONE, thread it into buildPlanFor's cfg (index.ts), matching the user's four season tags (#spring/#summer/#fall/#winter). Live weather (Open-Meteo) is the SEPARATE v2.0 layer (SPEC §236) — do not double-count.
design:
APPROVED DESIGN (2026-07-16 brainstorm). Three-part surgical change; season is DERIVED, never configured (no new env/config/schema).

1. New pure unit src/planner/season.ts: type Season = spring|summer|fall|winter; seasonForDate(date, timezone): reads month IN the IANA zone via Intl.DateTimeFormat({timeZone, month:'numeric'}), maps meteorological (3-5 spring, 6-8 summer, 9-11 fall, 12/1/2 winter). Returns the union (assignable to existing season?: string fields -> NO type ripple; keep fields as string per user).

2. Wiring src/index.ts rawBuildPlan (~line 294): add cfg.season = seasonForDate(new Date(), config.timezone). main() is composition root that owns the real clock (matches nowDate: () => new Date()). Derived once per real generation (past idempotency gate) from the WALL CLOCK at generation time (user chose clock over weekKey). buildPlan already flows cfg.season into BOTH composePools (hard filter) and buildPlannerInput currentSeason (soft bias) -- no other wiring.

3. Bug fix src/recipe-mcp/search.ts (~145-148): drop condition must be untagged-EXEMPT. Drop iff filters.season!==undefined && effectiveSeasons.length>0 && !effectiveSeasons.includes(filters.season). Untagged (empty season_tags) -> always eligible; tagged-not-now (e.g. Khao Soi winter/fall/spring in July) -> dropped; in-season -> retained+soft-boosted.

DECISIONS: (a) enforcement = hard-exclude out-of-season + soft bias, untagged exempt; (b) month->season = meteorological/month-based; (c) untagged always eligible. Corpus evidence: 100 recipes, 54 season-tagged, only 21 summer -> hard filter WITHOUT untagged-exempt would collapse pool to ~21 and drop year-round staples.

TESTS (TDD): season.test.ts (12 months, TZ-boundary UTC instant landing in different Chicago month, DST-adjacent); search.test.ts (untagged retained under filter, out-of-season dropped, in-season retained); build-plan.test.ts (derived season reaches both search filters and current_season).
acceptance_criteria: Current season is derived from the clock in MP_TIMEZONE and passed to the planner; a plan in a given season prefers/keeps season-tagged recipes and avoids off-season ones (e.g. no winter-only soup selected in July). Design decisions resolved: month->season mapping, and hard-filter vs soft-bias (note: hard-filter thins a small corpus and raises veg-floor pressure).
notes: Design/brainstorm before implementing: (1) month->season boundaries; (2) hard search 'season' filter vs soft current_season prompt bias vs both; (3) whether untagged-season recipes are always eligible (recommended, to avoid over-thinning). All the plumbing (search.SearchFilters.season, input.current_season, pools cfg.season) already exists — this is the derivation + wiring + the hard/soft policy.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #11 (squash-merged to main @ 9ff0fd8). v1.0 tag-based seasonality wired: pure seasonForDate() derives season from wall clock in MP_TIMEZONE; index.ts sets cfg.season; search.ts season filter made untagged-exempt. 474 tests green; live dry-run confirmed summer-leaning plan with Khao Soi excluded.

title: Slim the LLM extraction prompt: drop quality/season/effort now that NoteStore tags own them
description: Post q95.11, tags are authoritative for quality/season/effort/course/diet, but the ingest extraction prompt (extraction.ts) still asks the LLM to infer effort_tags/season_tags/quality. Removing them shrinks the prompt (cost win) and removes a now-unused inference. Requires an EXTRACTOR_VERSION bump + full re-extraction, and updating ExtractedFieldsSchema (make those fields optional/removed) and the sync merge (which currently falls back to LLM values when a tag is absent — decide whether to keep any fallback). Deferred from q95.11 to avoid the re-extraction there.
close_reason: Slimmed extraction: ExtractedFields={time,ingredients,veg_status}; prompt drops quality/season/effort (tags own them). EXTRACTOR_VERSION 1->2 (forces one re-extraction, batched before the full sync). search+get-recipe source season/effort/quality from tags; get-recipe now tag-consistent. 455 tests. Merged PR #10.

title: Type-check test files in CI/typecheck (tests currently excluded from tsc)
description: Surfaced during resume-quietly review (bd6.5): tsconfig.json excludes src/**/*.test.ts from tsc, and vitest.config.ts has no typecheck block — so test files are NEVER type-checked. This let a real type error slip (a resumeQuietly returning ActiveSession assigned to an incompatible OnStartupDeps signature 'passed' because the test wasn't type-checked). Add test typechecking: either a separate tsconfig (e.g. tsconfig.test.json including tests) run in a 'typecheck:test' script, or enable Vitest's typecheck mode (vitest --typecheck). Wire it into the acceptance gates so future type errors in tests are caught. Medium priority — improves the whole SDD loop's rigor.
close_reason: Test-file typechecking enabled: added tsconfig.test.json (includes tests, noEmit) + typecheck:test script, wired into CI. Surfaced + fixed 2 latent test-file type errors (generate.test.ts closure narrowing, search.test.ts missing ingredients). Part of CI setup commit.

title: Cap/backoff for permanently-failing note extraction + tighten sync failure handling
description: Follow-up from extraction review (q95.3, commit 9e1767b). (1) MODERATE: a note whose extraction deterministically fails re-attempts (1 initial + 1 repair = 2 LLM calls) on EVERY sync forever, no backoff/cap — because needs_review is a staleness OR-condition and the failed record rewrites content_hash to current. Add a per-note attempt cap or backoff (e.g. stop retrying after N consecutive failures until the body changes) to bound cost/noise. Does NOT cascade into the embed gate (independence holds). (2) MINOR: sync.ts failure try/catch wraps both extractRecipeFields AND the success-path upsertStructured — a DB write error after a SUCCESSFUL extraction is mislabeled as an extraction failure, discarding a good result; tighten the try to just the extraction call. (3) MINOR: the catch discards the error entirely — log a redacted reason (error.message is already proven secret-free) for triage. Non-blocking; extraction runs ~weekly.

title: Surface per-turn LLM error messages in the SDK harness
description: Follow-up from the Agent SDK harness review (7js.5, commit 42f09c9). The adapter only throws on a non-success FINAL result message; per-turn SDKAssistantMessage.error values (authentication_failed, billing_error, rate_limit, overloaded, server_error) are not inspected — a turn could error yet still contribute usage and let the loop reach a 'success' result, producing silent partial failures that are hard to debug. Inspect and surface per-turn errors (throw or propagate) before E2 extraction and E4 selection consume the harness. Also (nits from same review): drop the unnecessary optional-usage guard, and include SDKResultError.errors[] in the thrown message instead of just the subtype string.
close_reason: Per-turn SDKAssistantMessage.error now throws immediately (first-error-wins, named error type, no secrets); non-success final result includes SDKResultError.errors[]; dead usage guard dropped. TDD, all 4 gates green. Commit 481cd7f on build/phase-1-foundation.

title: DECISION: planner tuning params (vegFloorK, untestedRate, time-penalty)
description: ADR 0003 open item. Concrete values for vegFloorK, untestedRate, and total-time penalty weighting. DATA-DEPENDENT: tuned during the plan-quality validation pass (T4 tune) on the real corpus; code ships with config defaults until then. Gates T4 tune.

title: DECISION: seed-query strategy (single vs category-seeded multi-query)
description: ADR 0003 open item. Pool diversity: a single generic diversity seed vs category/cuisine-seeded multi-query. DATA-DEPENDENT: pick only if v1.0 pools come back homogeneous (a tuning knob, not a redesign). Gates T4 tune.

title: DECISION: operator re-run affordance for a failed week
description: ADR 0002 open item. When a week goes 'failed' (mid-flight crash), define the manual re-run mechanism: a --force regenerate flag, delete-the-row, or a small 're-run week W' command. Gates the re-run task (T3 rerun). Ratify: bd close --reason.
design: RECOMMENDATION: a 'bd-style' CLI subcommand on the daemon, e.g. 're-run <week_key> [--force]', that clears/overwrites the failed row and invokes generateForWeek with force=true. Reuses the dev force-regenerate path (T1 profile). Avoids raw row-deletion (error-prone) and keeps a clean audit. Ratify by closing.
close_reason: Ratified: failed-week recovery is a daemon CLI subcommand 're-run <week_key> [--force]' that clears/overwrites the failed row and calls generateForWeek(force=true), reusing the dev force-regenerate path (T1 profile). Avoids raw row-deletion; keeps a clean audit. Per ADR 0002 open item.

title: DECISION: active_max confidence threshold (fail-closed rule)
description: ADR 0001 open item. Pick the concrete time.confidence value below which a candidate FAILS active_max (fail-closed → not weeknight-eligible). DATA-DEPENDENT: resolved during the extraction-quality validation pass (T2 valid) against the real corpus — do not guess now. Gates T2 search + T2 valid.

title: DECISION: ingredient package_size capture (structure vs raw)
description: ADR 0001 open item / SPEC §5.1. count-with-package-size ('1 can (14 oz)', '2 (400 g) tins') — add a structured package_size field or leave in the raw line for v4.0 to parse. Gates the frozen schema (T2 schema) + extraction (T2 extract). Ratify: bd close --reason.
design: RECOMMENDATION: leave in raw for v1.0 (the doc's default when undecided). raw is always retained losslessly, so nothing is lost and v4.0 can parse or retrofit a structured field then — package_size is a v4.0 aggregation concern, not consumed in v1.0. Revisit at v4.0 grocery aggregation. Ratify by closing.
close_reason: Ratified: leave count-with-package-size in the raw ingredient line for v1.0 (ADR 0001 / SPEC §5.1 default). raw is retained losslessly, so v4.0 grocery aggregation can parse it or retrofit a structured package_size field then. Not consumed in v1.0.

title: Own API workspace/key + low-spend email alert
description: New Anthropic API key in its own workspace (independent visibility/revocability); metered per-token (subscription cap does NOT catch this). Low-spend email alert at a threshold well below alarming (a few $/month is a genuine anomaly). SPEC §9.3.

title: #agent-alerts wiring + local log
description: #agent-alerts is alerts-only (no heartbeat, no 'skipping this week'). Complete local log on disk. SPEC §9.4.

title: Plan-quality validation & parameter tuning
description: On the real corpus, tune vegFloorK, untestedRate, total-time penalty weighting, and seed-query strategy (single generic vs category-seeded multi-query if pools look homogeneous). ADR 0003 open items.
notes: Live full-corpus dry-run (2026-07-17, 764 food recipes) repeatedly hit weeknight-pool THINNESS: planner rationale 'only two rated non-meat options' forced untested 'try this' picks into regular slots. Root cause is likely the generic seed query (l7x) + small fanout limit (cookNights.constrained * fanoutMultiplier) under-filling the survivor pool after hard filters (active_max + main_dinner_only + season), NOT a data shortage (283 #dinner-tagged recipes exist). Tuning candidates: seed strategy (l7x), fanoutMultiplier / vegFloorK / untestedRate (kd5), and possibly a veg-aware fanout floor so the vegetarian-every-night constraint has enough rated options.

title: Enrich chosen via get_recipe
description: get_recipe(id) for the ~5-6 chosen (source links in v1.0; ingredient block load-bearing in v4.0). ADR 0003 pipeline step 4.
close_reason: get_recipe enrich + buildPlan entry complete: enrichPlan attaches Recipe/secondDishRecipe (concurrent, throws EnrichmentError on null), buildPlan chains composePools→buildPlannerInput→selectValidatedPlan→enrichPlan with SAME pools into select+validate, generic v1.0 seed. Commit 5b2d448. Review Approved (verified end-to-end).

title: Operator re-run affordance for failed/skipped week
description: A mechanism to manually re-run a week that went failed (mid-flight crash) — gated on the re-run-affordance decision. ADR 0002 open items.
close_reason: Operator re-run affordance complete: SessionStore.delete + reRunWeek (clears row then generateForWeek force:true; REFUSES non-failed rows w/o --force to prevent duplicate post — proven-tested) + parseReRunArgs. Commit 35f7d7e. Review Approved (safety guard proven; scope clean).

title: Resume-quietly on restart
description: Reload working_plan + thread_ts from SQLite and continue; say nothing in-thread (v3.0 listening). ADR 0002 / SPEC §8.
close_reason: resume-quietly complete: pure ActiveSession reconstitution (parse+validate working_plan against composed EnrichedWeekPlanSchema, ResumeError on malformed, zero side effects). Commits 7502d37 + 02d72c3 (fix: generic JSON-parse msg no secret leak; OnStartupDeps.resumeQuietly →void so seam type-checks, negative-control verified; top-level .strict()). Review Approved after fix. Schema-dedup→bd6.8.

title: Extraction-quality validation on real corpus
description: Run the extraction pass on the actual recipe corpus; eyeball confidence/needs_review; pick the concrete active_max confidence threshold for the fail-closed rule. Build-time validation, not a design unknown. ADR 0001 consequences/open items.

title: get_recipe tool (full; chosen only)
description: Return full Recipe = RecipeCandidate + ingredients[] (frozen schema) + body + source_note_id. Called only for the ~5-6 chosen. ADR 0001 D2.
close_reason: get_recipe complete: full Recipe assembly (note meta + structured fields incl. ingredient block), RecipeSchema-validated, null for unknown/not-ready. Added VectorStore.getNote getter. Commit 92218b7. Review Approved (nits only).

title: Cost Control & Ops/Alerting
description: Cost enforcement and operability (SPEC §9.3, §9.4). Owns: in-code dollar-denominated cost tracking (tokens returned per response → cost via per-model rates in config), the per-run generation hard cap (~$2 / ~500K tokens; trips only on a true runaway), the Anthropic low-spend email alert, the #agent-alerts channel wiring (alerts-only: no heartbeat), and a complete local log on disk. Turn/thread caps are v3.0 but the tracking infra + generation cap are v1.0. Hooks into E3 (counters on session row) and E4 (generation token tracking). Source: SPEC §9.3, §9.4.

title: Pair a side dish with a main (e.g. burgers + potato salad)
description: q95.11 excludes #side recipes from being standalone dinners and captures is_side on candidates. Next: optionally PAIR a side with a compatible main in the plan (the user's real use — 'we make this with burgers'). Design: a side pool + a pairing step (LLM or heuristic) that attaches an optional side to selected mains; schema/WeekPlan additions for a paired side; render + grocery implications. Data (is_side) already captured.

title: CostMeter: apply cache-aware input rates (cache-read cheaper than fresh input)
description: Post-q95.10, agent-sdk-client counts all input categories (input_tokens + cache_creation + cache_read) at the single inputPerMTok rate — a deliberate conservative over-estimate for the cost cap. For more accurate accounting/reporting, model the real Anthropic rates: cache-write ~1.25x input, cache-read ~0.1x input. Requires threading the cache-token breakdown through LlmResult.usage (currently flattened to inputTokens/outputTokens) into CostMeter. Low priority: current behavior is safe (never undercounts) and cache reads are rare across distinct notes.
acceptance_criteria: CostMeter cost reflects cache-write/cache-read rate tiers; LlmResult.usage carries the cache breakdown; cap still errs conservative.

title: Promote EnrichedWeekPlanSchema into enrich.ts (dedupe from resume.ts)
description: From resume-quietly review (bd6.5, commit 7502d37): resume.ts composes an EnrichedWeekPlanSchema/EnrichedMealSchema (from SelectedMealSchema + RecipeSchema) locally because enrich.ts exports no schema. It's correct and anchored to real types, but is a second independently-maintained description of EnrichedWeekPlan that will drift if EnrichedMeal/EnrichedWeekPlan change. Promote the schema into enrich.ts (its owner) as the single source of truth and have resume.ts (+ any v3.0 listener) import it. Low priority; do when a second consumer appears.

title: generateForWeek robustness follow-ups (failed-write defense, force+existing-row, error sanitization)
description: Deferred Low findings from generateForWeek review (bd6.3, commit 09184a3). (1) The pre-post failure path's own transition-to-failed write is undefended: if that update throws, the original buildPlan/post error is lost and alert() never fires — consider guarding it. (2) force+existing-row: expirePriorIfUncommitted runs and can mutate the prior week before store.insert throws the PK constraint — bd6.6 (operator re-run) owns clean force-overwrite; add a test/comment when it lands. (3) alert()/error messages forward raw error text from buildPlan/post (E4/E5) — state an explicit no-secret contract for those implementations, or sanitize at the alert boundary. All Low; non-blocking.
